description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing female figure, likely representing the Virgin Mary, holding a small child in her left arm and accompanied by a kneeling child at her right side. The figures are rendered in a classical style, with flowing drapery and detailed facial features. The main figure wears a long robe and a head covering, while the children are dressed in simple garments. The sculpture is carved from a light-colored stone, possibly marble, and stands on a rectangular plinth. The back of the sculpture shows the drapery cascading down in naturalistic folds, with some weathering visible. The overall composition conveys a sense of tenderness and reverence, typical of religious statuary.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of a robed woman holding a child, with another child kneeling at her side, rendered in a classical, religious style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the woman holding a child in her left arm and a kneeling child at her right, with detailed facial expressions and drapery.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the main figure and the child in her arms, with the drapery's depth and folds visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the figures, with the drapery flowing down and some surface weathering apparent.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the kneeling child and the side profile of the main figure, showing the interaction between the figures.
attribute_subject_matter: religious figures, likely the Virgin Mary with children
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
